title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout with Dynamic Row Height Calculation

statement.markdown: You are given a CSS grid layout with a dynamic number of rows and columns. The grid layout is defined by a 2D array where each element represents a grid cell. The value of each element is a string representing the CSS style of the cell. The style string contains the height of the cell in pixels as a decimal number. Your task is to calculate the total height of the grid layout by summing up the heights of all cells. However, there are some bugs in the given code that need to be fixed.
statement.inputFormat: A 2D array of style strings representing the grid layout
statement.outputFormat: A number representing the total height of the grid layout in pixels
statement.notes: The input grid layout is guaranteed to be rectangular, i.e., all rows have the same number of columns.

constraints.description: The number of rows in the grid layout
The number of columns in the grid layout

examples.explanation: The total height of the grid layout is the sum of the heights of all cells, which is 100 + 200 + 50 = 350
The total height of the grid layout is the sum of the heights of all cells, which is 300 + 150 + 100 = 550

hints.text: First hint text: Pay attention to the CSS style strings and extract the height values correctly.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout with Dynamic Row Height Calculation
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: The key to solving this problem is to extract the height values from the CSS style strings and sum them up correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: One possible approach is to iterate over each cell in the grid layout, extract the height value from the CSS style string, and add it to a running total.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to extract the height values from the CSS style strings and sum them up correctly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The optimal solution is correct because it correctly extracts the height values from the CSS style strings and sums them up correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1. Iterate over each cell in the grid layout. 2. Use a regular expression to extract the height value from the CSS style string. 3. Add the height value to a running total.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not extracting the height values correctly.
